text.title: cqrs-pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: ≥3 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + detector + root-cause + fix.

failure-mode.description: Commands return data to the client
failure-mode.detector: command returns the persisted entity OR query writes/logs OR single read model serves divergent access patterns
failure-mode.repair: This couples the response to the write model and breaks eventual consistency. The client expects to see the written data immediately, which is only true if consistency is strong. This makes the system behave unpredictably under load or network lag.

failure-mode.detector: command returns the persisted entity OR query writes/logs OR single read model serves divergent access patterns
failure-mode.repair: Queries become unpredictable, unsafe to retry, and hard to cache. If a query increments a counter, retrying it produces wrong results. Queries should be pure functions.

failure-mode.description: Single read model serving all queries
failure-mode.detector: command returns the persisted entity OR query writes/logs OR single read model serves divergent access patterns
failure-mode.repair: The read model becomes a kitchen sink, storing every field anyone might need. Queries become slow because they scan unnecessary data. CQRS's power is multiple optimized read models.
